description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, test, check, and deploy, using a global before_script that exports PATH, creates the R library directory, updates apt, installs the APT_DEPS packages, configures the CRAN repository, sets Ncpus, installs the remotes package, updates all package dependencies, and installs stage‑specific R packages via remotes::install_cran using the R_STAGE_DEPS variable. Define a cache keyed by ${CI_JOB_NAME} that stores the R library path ${R_LIBS}. In the test stage add four jobs—test:devel:clang, test:devel:gcc, test:patched:gcc, and test:release:gcc—each extending the .test:rpkg template, using images rhub/debian-clang-devel:latest, rhub/debian-gcc-devel:latest, rhub/debian-gcc-patched:latest, and rhub/debian-gcc-release:latest respectively, and each runs "mkdir -p ${R_CHECK_DIR}", "R CMD build ${R_BUILD_ARGS} .", and "R CMD check ${R_CHECK_ARGS} -o ${R_CHECK_DIR} $(ls -1t *.tar.gz | head -n 1)"; they produce artifacts named ${CI_JOB_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME} containing ${R_CHECK_DIR}/*.Rcheck and always upload them. In the check stage add a code:coverage job (image rhub/debian-gcc-release:latest) that needs test:release:gcc and test:devel:gcc, allows failure, reports coverage with the regex '/Coverage: \d+\.\d+\%/', runs on success, and executes "Rscript -e 'covr::codecov(type = "all", quiet = FALSE)'". Also add a code:lint job (same image) that needs test:release:gcc, triggers only on changes to R/*.{r,R} and inst/tinytest/*.{r,R}, allows failure, runs on success, and executes "Rscript -e 'lintr::lint_package()'". Finally, in the deploy stage add a pages job (image rhub/debian-gcc-release:latest) that needs test:release:gcc, runs only on the master branch, runs "R CMD build ${R_BUILD_ARGS} .", "R CMD INSTALL ${R_INSTALL_ARGS} $(ls -1t *.tar.gz | head -n 1)", "Rscript -e 'pkgdown::build_site(devel = TRUE)'", creates the directory public/src/contrib, and runs "Rscript -e 'drat::insertPackage(commandArgs(TRUE), "public")' $(ls -1t *.tar.gz | head -n 1)", then publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact.
